entries.text:
Official DELO MONOPOX TC2270 Technical Datasheet fallback extracted from web-visible DELO TDS text because the direct PDF endpoint returned HTTP 404 to local automation on 2026-04-24 while search-index text exposed the official current document.

Product: DELO MONOPOX TC2270. Datasheet state: as of 28.10.2024 07:58, Page 1-4. Chemistry: modified epoxy resin, one-component, heat-curing, free of solvents, thermally conductive and electrically insulating. Special features: electronic adhesive; RoHS Directive 2015/863/EU compliant; halogen-free according to IEC 61249-2-21; reliable according to JEDEC MSL 1 referring to IPC/JEDEC J-STD-020D.1; compliant with limits of VOC content in adhesive according to GB33372-2020. Typical area of use: -40 to 150 C.

Curing and processing: typical curing time 90 min at +60 C in air convection oven, or 15 min at +80 C in air convection oven. Conditioning time typical 0.5 h when stored in cold conditions in containers up to 10 ml. Processing time in standard climate +23 C / 50% r.h. is 48 h. Storage life in unopened original container is 6 months at -25 C to -15 C. Color in cured condition in 1 mm layer thickness is gray; transparency in cured condition in 1 mm layer thickness is opaque.

Filler and liquid properties: filler information aluminum nitride. Density 2.15 g/cm3 by DELO Standard 13, liquid. Viscosity 145000 mPa.s, liquid, rheometer, shear rate 10 1/s, gap 500 um. Thixotropy index 4.5, liquid, rheometer, gap 500 um.

Strength and mechanical properties after 80 C x 30 min cure unless stated otherwise: compression shear strength 20 MPa by DELO Standard 5 on ABS/ABS; 34 MPa by DELO Standard 5 on FR4/FR4 with annealing pretreatment; 11 MPa by DELO Standard 5 on LCP MR25/LCP MR25. Die shear strength 60 N by DELO Standard 30 on 1 mm x 1 mm silicon chip to gold on 25 mm x 15 mm plate; 62 N by DELO Standard 30 on 1 mm x 1 mm silicon chip to FR4, 20 mm x 20 mm x 5 mm, annealed. Tensile strength 28 MPa by DIN EN ISO 527. Elongation at tear 11% by DIN EN ISO 527. Young's modulus 18000 MPa by DMTA. Shore hardness D 88 by DIN EN ISO 868. Glass transition temperature 40 C by DMTA. Coefficient of linear expansion 98 ppm/K by DELO Standard 26 TMA, evaluation 70 C to 120 C. Shrinkage 2.8 vol.%.

Electrical, thermal and water properties: water absorption 0.1 wt.% by DIN EN ISO 62, 80 C x 30 min, distilled water, 24 h. Specific thermal conductivity 1.7 W/(m.K) by ASTM D5470 criteria after 80 C x 30 min cure. Volume resistivity >1E14 Ohm.cm by DIN EN 62631-3-1 after 80 C x 30 min cure. Surface resistance >1E13 Ohm by DIN EN 62631-3-2 after 80 C x 30 min cure.

General curing and processing information: published curing time was determined in the laboratory and can vary with adhesive quantity and component geometry. Heating time of components must be added to actual curing time and depends on component size and heat input. Specified curing temperature must be reached directly at the adhesive. Increasing or decreasing cure temperature, irradiation intensity or cure time shortens or prolongs cure and can change physical properties. Depending on adhesive quantity, exothermic reaction heat is generated and can lead to overheating; in this case select a lower curing temperature. Values measured after 24 h at approx. 23 C / 50% r.h. unless otherwise specified.

Specification note: the Technical Datasheet states values are typical laboratory values, do not constitute a product specification, and suitability must be tested by the customer for the intended application.

Official EPO-TEK 301 Technical Data Sheet OCR fallback. The official PDF downloaded successfully, but its local text layer only exposed footer text, so this cache preserves OCR-backed text from rendered pages of the official August 2024 Rev XIV PDF.

Product: EPO-TEK 301, Spectrally Transparent Epoxy. Date August 2024, Rev XIV. No. of components: two. Mix ratio by weight: 20:5. Specific gravity: Part A 1.15, Part B 0.87. Pot life: 1-2 hours. Shelf life bulk: one year at room temperature. Recommended cure: 65 C / 2 hours. Minimum alternative cures, which may not achieve listed performance: 65 C / 1 hour or 23 C / 24 hours.

Notes: containers should be kept closed when not in use. Filled systems should be stirred thoroughly before mixing and prior to use. Performance properties including rheology and conductivity may vary when bi-pak or syringe packaging or post-processing is performed. Syringe packaging can impact initial viscosity and effective pot life. Total mass should not exceed 25 grams.

Product description: two-component, room-temperature-curing epoxy featuring very low viscosity and excellent optical-mechanical properties. Physical properties: color before cure Part A clear/colorless, Part B clear/colorless. Consistency pourable liquid. Viscosity at 23 C and 100 rpm is 100-200 cP. Thixotropic index N/A. Glass transition temperature >=65 C. Coefficient of thermal expansion below Tg 39 x 10^-6 in/in C and above Tg 98 x 10^-6 in/in C. Shore D hardness 85. Lap shear at 23 C >2,000 psi. Die shear at 23 C >=10 kg and 3,556 psi. Degradation temperature 430 C. Weight loss: 0.12% at 200 C, 0.13% at 250 C, 0.39% at 300 C. Suggested operating temperature <300 C intermittent. Storage modulus 436,249 psi. Particle size N/A.

Electrical and thermal properties: thermal conductivity N/A. Volume resistivity at 23 C >=1 x 10^13 ohm-cm. Dielectric constant at 1 kHz 4.00. Dissipation factor at 1 kHz 0.016.

Optical properties at 23 C: spectral transmission >=99% at 382-980 nm, >=97% at 980-1,640 nm, >=95% at 1,640-2,040 nm. Refractive index 1.519 at 589 nm.

Suggested application notes: semiconductor optical glob top or underfill; adhesion to common wafer passivation, solder mask and flex circuits; compatible with LED die, Si and GaAs. PCB general potting and protection over FR4, flex or ceramic PCBs. Fiber optic adhesive for glass and plastic fibers, wicking into fiber bundles used in patch cords or sensor devices, adhesive/seal/encapsulant for fiber packaging and components, transmission of IR up to 2500 nm, terminating fibers into ferrules, fiber coupling and splicing. Optoelectronic LCD/LED adhesive for laminating glass layers, adhesion to PET plastic, general potting, encapsulation and protection, spectral transmission in VIS and IR light, adhesive/encapsulant for VCSEL packaged devices, resisting yellowing per ASTM D1925, adhesive for precision optics including lens, prism, beam splitter cubes, mirrors and diodes. NASA approved low-outgassing epoxy. For ISO 10993 biocompatible version see EPO-TEK MED-301.

Official Dow fallback extracted from the web-visible DOWSIL Silicone Sealants and Foams for Industrial, Appliance and Maintenance selection guide, Form No. 80-8283-01, when direct local download returned HTTP 403.

Product: DOWSIL Q3-3526 Base and Catalyst Adhesive. Category: Table IV neutral, two-component and one-part oxime neutral-cure sealants. Chemistry / cure: two-component neutral silicone adhesive/sealant; VMQ sealant type. Special features: fast-curing, non-slump, two-part silicone adhesive/sealant. Primary uses/applications: assembly of automotive headlights and auxiliary lights, body panels and body components, oven door windows and other appliance components.

Exact table values for Q3-3526: temperature range continuous -50 to 190 C; skin-over time 8 min; tack-free time 20 min; flow rate <2 mm; durometer Shore A 38-40; tensile strength 2 MPa; elongation 270-280%; specific gravity 1.36-1.32; listings/spec none; colors gray and black; fluid-resistance sealant type VMQ.

Primerless adhesion ratings for Q3-3526: nylon 6/6 Excellent; polycarbonate Excellent; glass Excellent; aluminum mill finish Excellent; copper Excellent; galvanized steel Excellent; low carbon steel Excellent; stainless steel Excellent. Acrylic, ABS, LDPE and polypropylene are marked with an asterisk in the Dow table, meaning consult the local Dow representative for further advice on adhesion properties.

Dow footnotes: most paints will not adhere to sealant; not for underwater structural or adhesive applications; requires atmospheric moisture to cure; may stress-crack some plastics, test before use. Estimated service temperatures are based on product formulation and laboratory testing; actual service temperature range depends on application environment. Provenance note: this cache entry is a controlled text fallback because the official PDF endpoint was blocked during automated fetch, but the PDF text is web-visible through Dow's document endpoint.

Official Dow DOWSIL EA-9187L UV Silicone Conformal Coating TDS fallback extracted from Dow Form No. 11-3909-01 because dow.com product/document endpoints block or no longer expose this PDF to local automation; the mirrored PDF identifies Dow copyright/trademark and unrestricted sharing.

Product: DOWSIL EA-9187L UV Silicone Conformal Coating. Form No. 11-3909-01, 2018. Description: one-part medium-viscosity conformal coating that is UL approved. Features: controlled volatile content to avoid electric failure; no heating, no exothermic heat during cure, no solvents; excellent unprimed adhesion to many substrates; faster in-line processing at room temperature with optional heat acceleration; UV indicator; UL 746C and UL 94 V-0 specification.

Applications and methods: flowable one-component neutral-cure material designed for manufacture of electrical and electronic products and components requiring high reliability, UL 746C and UL 94 V-0. Application methods: spray, brush, flow, automated pattern coating and dip coating with special precautions.

Typical properties from the TDS: one part; appearance translucent; viscosity 900 mPa.s; skin-over time at 25 C 9 min; specific gravity cured 0.98; durometer 17 Shore A; tensile strength 0.44 MPa; elongation 165%; unprimed lap shear on glass 0.31 MPa; dielectric strength 20 kV/mm; volume resistivity 3E+15 ohm.cm; dielectric constant at 1 MHz 2.8; dissipation factor at 1 MHz 0.0009; UL flammability classification 94 V-0; agency listing UL 746C; storage condition 0-30 C; shelf life 360 days.

Processing and cure: solventless RTV elastomeric conformal coatings require atmospheric moisture to cure. Cure depends on application method, film thickness, temperature and humidity. Skin-over time is a non-tacky-surface indication; full cure times develop full physical properties such as durometer, tensile strength or adhesion and can be improved with mild heat of 60 C or less. Pot life depends on application method; minimize ambient moisture exposure with dry air or dry nitrogen blanketing when possible.

Adhesion and repair: adhesion can lag behind cure and may take up to 72 h at room temperature to build; test adhesion after 72 h. Apply to clean and dry substrates. Difficult low-surface-energy surfaces may need priming or chemical/plasma etching. The coating can be removed by scraping, cutting, solvents or stripping agents. A soldering iron may be applied through the coating to replace a component with proper ventilation, followed by cleaning, drying and recoating.

Temperature, storage and limitations: Dow states most silicone adhesives are operational from -45 to 200 C for long periods, with possible thermal cycling to -55 C if verified on the user's assembly. Keep containers tightly closed, minimize headspace, purge partially filled containers with dry air or nitrogen, and prevent moisture contact. Multiple packaging sizes are available. Not tested or represented for medical or pharmaceutical uses. Typical values are not for specification writing.

Official Parker LORD fallback extracted from web-visible technical data snippets when direct PDF download returned HTTP 403.

Product: FUSOR 2098 Crash Durable Structural Adhesive (Slow).
Technical data sheet positioning: two-component, epoxy-based structural metal bonding adhesive providing OEM-strength and crash-durable repairs.
Applications called out in official snippet: weld bonding, rivet bonding, and bonding of replacement structural and non-structural auto body panels.
Official benefit language in snippet: durable, higher impact strength performance, restores OEM NVH and crash durability.
Handling guidance from official snippet: pre-fit new panel, plan mechanical fastening and clamp locations, drill rivet holes as needed, clean bonding surfaces with solvent before adhesive application.
Interpretation for Glueboy: collision-repair structural epoxy for replacement body panel workflows; not a general consumer adhesive.
Provenance note: this cache entry is a controlled text fallback because Parker blocks direct automated access to the PDF endpoint.

Official Parker LORD fallback extracted from web-visible technical data snippets when local automation encountered HTTP 403.

Product: Fusor 108B/109B Metal Bonding Adhesive (Medium).
Chemistry: two-component acrylic-based metal bonding adhesive.
Applications: panel bonding, weld bonding and rivet bonding of properly prepared quarter panels, rear body panels, roof panels, door skins, van side panels and outer truck bed panels.
Features and benefits: fast room-temperature snap cure, reduced cycle times, bonds bare metal / steel / aluminum, can be riveted and resistance welded, acrylic chemistry provides strong environmental resistance.
Typical properties from official TDS snippet: appearance green paste; base chemistry acrylic; work time 40 minutes at 70 F / 21 C; clamp time 2 hours; cure time 4 hours.
Bond performance values shown in official TDS snippet include room-temperature lap shear up to 2067 psi on ground CRS and 2029 psi on ground aluminum, plus elevated-temperature and low-temperature shear data and T-peel data on common automotive metals.
Provenance note: this cache entry preserves the official TDS content in text form for reliable autonomous runs.

Official Parker LORD fallback extracted from web-visible technical data snippets when direct PDF download returned HTTP 403.

Product: Fusor 112B Metal Bonding Adhesive (Slow).
Chemistry: two-component acrylic-based metal bonding adhesive with extended open time for larger panel repairs and warmer shop conditions.
Handling guidance from official snippet: apply 3/8 to 1/2 inch bead to prepared mating surfaces; maintain contact during cure; use screws or rivets in hard-to-clamp areas; if bond-only, clamps or temporary fasteners may be removed after 3 hours at 70 F / 21 C; full room-temperature cure requires 8 hours.
Shelf life / storage: 12 months at 75 F / 24 C in original unopened container.
Bond performance from official TDS snippet: lap shear strength ASTM D1002 on cold rolled steel 2100 psi (14.5 MPa), cohesive failure; on aluminum 2700 psi (18.6 MPa), cohesive failure. Torsional impact resistance GM 9751P on both cold rolled steel and aluminum is listed as greater than 106 in-lb with no adhesive failure.
Provenance note: this cache entry is a controlled text fallback because Parker blocks direct automated access to the PDF endpoint.

Official Parker LORD fallback extracted from the curated TDS record and web-visible technical data snippets when direct PDF download returned HTTP 403.

Product: Fusor 142 Plastic Repair Adhesive (Fast).
Chemistry: two-component urethane-based plastic repair adhesive.
Applications from the official technical data sheet record: structural and cosmetic repair of bumpers, bumper tabs, and interior or exterior plastic components; compatible repair context includes SMC, fiberglass panels, e-coat, and primed or painted metals.
Appearance / form: black paste in cartridge format.
Handling properties from official TDS record: work time 1 minute at 70 F / 21 C; clamp time 10 minutes; sand time 20 minutes; paint time 30 minutes; full cure 24 hours.
Repair-procedure cautions from official TDS record: structural plastic repairs require a backer patch or reinforcing mesh; surface preparation requires Fusor 703 adhesion prep/cleaner and a light coat of Fusor 602EZ surface modifier before adhesive application.
Interpretation for Glueboy: fast automotive plastic repair urethane, not a general panel-bonding adhesive and not an electronics adhesive.
Provenance note: this cache entry is a controlled text fallback because Parker blocks direct automated access to the PDF endpoint.

Official Parker LORD fallback extracted from web-visible technical data snippets when direct PDF download returned HTTP 403.

Product: Fusor 152 Plastic Repair Adhesive (Medium).
Chemistry: two-component urethane-based plastic repair adhesive.
Applications from official snippet: structural and cosmetic repair of bumper covers, bumper tabs and other interior or exterior plastic components; also used with SMC and fiberglass repair contexts in Parker literature.
Key handling note in official snippet: structural plastic repairs require a backer patch to provide strength.
Official positioning from snippet: medium-speed plastic repair adhesive for collision-repair workflows, suitable where a sandable feather edge and longer repair window are useful.
Interpretation for Glueboy: repair adhesive for plastic automotive parts rather than a metal structural bonder or electronics adhesive.
Provenance note: this cache entry is a controlled text fallback because Parker blocks direct automated access to the PDF endpoint.

Official Dow fallback extracted from the DOWSIL TC-2035 Adhesive technical data sheet when direct automation returned HTTP 403.

Product: DOWSIL TC-2035 Adhesive.
Description: two-part heat-cure silicone thermally conductive adhesive with low bond-line thickness.
Chemistry and cure: addition-cure silicone; heat cure; no cure by-products; cures in deep sections and confined assemblies. Mix ratio 1:1 by weight or volume. Cure guidance: 100 C or above; 30 min at 125 C; 10 min at 150 C; thick-section pre-cure starting point 30 min at 70 C.
Handling: working time at 25 C 4 h by viscosity after 4 h; open time at 25 C 2 h by lap shear adhesion. Viscosity CTM 1094 at 10 1/s: Part A 130,000 cP, Part B 118,000 cP, mixed 125,000 cP. Mixed thixotropy 2.8.
Thermal: thermal conductivity CTM 1163A transient method 3.3 W/m.K. Thermal resistance ASTM D5470: 0.25 C/W at 50 um and 0.44 C/W at 100 um. Linear CTE ASTM E831 -50 to 200 C: 92 ppm/K. Heat capacity DIN 51007: 0.83 J/g.C at 25 C, 0.98 at 100 C, 1.09 at 150 C.
Electrical: dielectric strength CTM 0114A at 1 mm is 533 V/mil / 21 kV/mm. Dielectric constant CTM 0112: 6 at 100 Hz and 5.9 at 100 kHz. Volume resistivity CTM 0249A: 5.5E+15 ohm.cm. Dissipation factor: 7E-03 at 100 Hz and 4E-03 at 100 kHz.
Mechanical: tensile strength CTM 0137 522 psi / 3.6 MPa. Elongation 43%. Lap shear CTM 0243A: aluminum 381 psi / 2.63 MPa; copper 416 psi / 2.87 MPa. Hardness: 95 Shore A JIS Type A / CTM 0099M; 45 Shore D CTM 0793D / ASTM D2240. Wet density 3.0 g/cm3.
Applications: bonding organic and ceramic substrates such as PCB, HDI and DBC to heat sinks for transmission modules, power modules and conversion modules.
Cautions: good adhesion is not expected on non-reactive metal substrates or non-reactive plastics such as Teflon, polyethylene or polypropylene without treatment/primer; avoid solvent exposure where possible; not tested or represented for medical or pharmaceutical uses.
Provenance note: this cache entry is a controlled text fallback because the official Dow PDF endpoint returned HTTP 403 to local automation.

Official Chip Quik fallback extracted from web-visible EGS10C-20G datasheet when local automation received HTTP 403.

Product: EGS10C-20G Electronics Grade Silicone Adhesive Sealant (Clear) 20g squeeze tube. Cure: skin 10 min, full cure 24 h for 1/8 in bead, max adhesion 7 days at 25 C / 50% RH. Operating range -57 C to +204 C, intermittent +232 C. Hardness 30 +/-2 Shore A. Tensile 250 +/-25 psi. Elongation 400 +/-25%. Tear 30 +/-10 psi. Peel adhesion 8 +/-2 psi primed aluminum, 10 +/-2 psi glass, 12 +/-2 psi primed mortar. Dielectric strength 460 V/mil / 18 kV/mm. Dielectric constant 2.7 at 50 Hz, 1 kHz and 1 MHz. Dissipation factor 0.0009 at 50 Hz, 0.0004 at 1 kHz, 0.0002 at 1 MHz. Volume resistivity 2 x 10^14, surface resistivity 3 x 10^16. Viscosity 750,000-2,000,000 mPa.s. Standards: ASTM C920, MIL 46106B Type 1, RoHS 3, REACH.

Official Chip Quik fallback extracted from web-visible NCS10C-20G datasheet when local automation received HTTP 403.

Product: NCS10C-20G Neutral Cure Silicone Adhesive Sealant (Clear) 20g squeeze tube. Neutral cure RTV silicone. Bonds aluminum, vinyl, fiberglass, ceramics, brick, mortar, concrete, glass, granite, marble, metals, wood, plastic, porcelain, PVC and steel. Meets ASTM C920, TT-S-00230C, TT-S-001543A, MIL-46106 Type 1. Operating range -46 C to +204 C; intermittent +232 C. Cure: skin 10 min, full cure 24 h, max adhesion 7 days at 25 C / 50% RH. Viscosity 750,000-2,000,000 mPa.s. Hardness 30 Shore A. Tensile 250 psi. Tear 30 psi. Elongation 400%. Dielectric strength 460 V/mil / 18 kV/mm. Life expectancy under normal conditions 30+ years.

Product: HPS10C-20G High Performance Silicone 100% RTV Silicone (Clear) 20g squeeze tube. One-component moisture cure. Operating range -46 C to +204 C. Application temperature range -37 C to +65 C. Cure at 21 C / 50% RH: 10 min skin, 24 h full cure, 7 days max adhesion. Viscosity 582,000 mPa.s. Hardness 25 Shore A. Tensile 264 psi. Elongation 500%. Size 20 g. Standards: ASTM C920, TT-S-00230C, TT-S-001543A, MIL-46106 Type 1. Bonds glass, metal, ceramic, most paints and most plastics.

Official Chip Quik fallback extracted from web-visible AD1-10S datasheet when local automation received HTTP 403.

Product: AD1-10S Thermoset Chip Glue (Red), 10 cc / 10 g syringe. Heat-curing epoxy adhesive for bonding surface mount chips and ICs to PCBs; higher viscosity for stencil printing. Cure: 90-120 sec at 150 C or higher; recommended 150-260 C; max 260 C; designed for leaded and lead-free reflow. Operating range after curing -40 C to +125 C. Lap shear after curing >15 MPa steel-steel at 25 C. Density 1.25 g/cc. Viscosity 450,000 mPa.s. Tg 80 C. CTE below Tg 60 ppm/C; above Tg 120 ppm/C. Thermal conductivity 0.14 W/m.K. Dielectric strength 25 kV/mm. Dielectric constant 3.2 at 25 C and 1 MHz. Dielectric loss tangent <0.02. Spot diameter growth <10%. Stencil life >8 h at 20-50% RH and >4 h at 50-70% RH.

Official Chip Quik fallback extracted from web-visible AD7-5S / AD7 family datasheets when local automation received HTTP 403.

Product: AD7 Thermoset Chip Bonding Epoxy (Red), sold as 5 g / 5 cc syringe, 10 g / 10 cc syringe, 30 g / 30 cc syringe and 200 g / 6 oz cartridge. Heat-curing epoxy adhesive designed to bond surface-mount chips and ICs to PCBs; low viscosity; dot/line dispensing; helps hold large or heavy surface mount components during two-sided reflow. Cure: 120-180 sec at 190 C or higher; recommended 190-260 C; max 260 C; designed for Sn63/Pb37 and SAC305 reflow temperatures. Maximum dot size 10 mm x 10 mm x 1 mm, 100 mm3. Operating range after curing -40 C to +125 C. Density 1.4 g/cc. Viscosity 44 Pa.s / 44,000 mPa.s. Stencil life >12 h at 20-50% RH and >8 h at 50-70% RH. AD7 only works as adhesive after reflow heat cure and cooling.

Official Chip Quik fallback extracted from web-visible SG1-0.5 / SG1-1.0 datasheets when local automation received HTTP 403.

Product: SG1 Super Glue - Thin Viscosity - Penetrating cyanoacrylate glue. Product highlights: thin viscosity penetrating super glue for penetrating tight-fitting joints. Bonds metal, plastic, wood, cardboard, paper, ceramic, leather and more. Specifications: viscosity 5 cP / 5 mPa.s; curing time 3 seconds; main ingredient cyanoacrylate; thermal conductivity after curing 0.2 W/m.K; operating temperature after curing -30 C to +90 C; size 15 mL squeeze bottle for SG1-0.5 and 30 mL squeeze bottle for SG1-1.0. Storage: store at 3-25 C, do not freeze, allow 4 h to reach operating temperature 20-25 C before use. Shelf life >24 months unopened. Transportation: no shipping restrictions when total shipment volume of Super Glue does not exceed 1000 mL; normal ground or air transit below 0 C or above 25 C will not impact stated shelf life.

Official Chip Quik fallback extracted from web-visible SG2-0.5 / SG2-1.0 datasheets when local automation received HTTP 403.

Product: SG2 Super Glue - Medium Viscosity - Gap Filling cyanoacrylate glue. Product highlights: medium viscosity gap filling super glue for joints that will not fit perfectly tight. Bonds metal, plastic, wood, cardboard, paper, ceramic, leather and more. Specifications: viscosity 100 cP / 100 mPa.s; curing time 15 seconds; main ingredient cyanoacrylate; thermal conductivity after curing 0.2 W/m.K; operating temperature after curing -30 C to +90 C; size 15 mL squeeze bottle for SG2-0.5 and 30 mL squeeze bottle for SG2-1.0. Storage: store at 3-25 C, do not freeze, allow 4 h to reach operating temperature 20-25 C before use. Shelf life >24 months unopened. Transportation: no shipping restrictions when total shipment volume of Super Glue does not exceed 1000 mL; normal ground or air transit below 0 C or above 25 C will not impact stated shelf life.

Official Chip Quik fallback extracted from web-visible SG3-0.5 / SG3-1.0 datasheets when local automation received HTTP 403.

Product: SG3 Super Glue - Thick Viscosity - Big Gap Filling cyanoacrylate glue. Product highlights: thick viscosity large gap filling super glue for joints that will not fit tight. Bonds metal, plastic, wood, cardboard, paper, ceramic, leather and more. Specifications: viscosity 700 cP / 700 mPa.s; curing time 45 seconds; main ingredient cyanoacrylate; thermal conductivity after curing 0.2 W/m.K; operating temperature after curing -30 C to +90 C; size 15 mL squeeze bottle for SG3-0.5 and 30 mL squeeze bottle for SG3-1.0. Storage: store at 3-25 C, do not freeze, allow 4 h to reach operating temperature 20-25 C before use. Shelf life >24 months unopened. Transportation: no shipping restrictions when total shipment volume of Super Glue does not exceed 1000 mL; normal ground or air transit below 0 C or above 25 C will not impact stated shelf life.

Product: 10-114 Quik Stik. Description: two-part unfilled epoxy adhesive designed for high-speed bonding of metal, ceramics and most plastics. It cures to a tough semi-rigid material and is free flowing in viscosity. It gives good resistance to water, salt spray, inorganic acids and bases and most organic solvents. Formulated to a 1:1 mix ratio. Cure is normally achieved at room temperature within 20-30 minutes, with full cure in 24 hours. Elevated-temperature cure can be used to reach final properties quickly, but no schedule is stated. Features: clear color; 3-5 minutes pot life, 20 g mass; 80 Shore D hardness; 13,000 cP estimated mixed viscosity; 1.16 specific gravity mixed; 2,800 psi tensile strength / lap shear (Al/Al); 410 V/mil dielectric strength; -40 to 130 C temperature range, 130 C equals 266 F; 12 months shelf life from date of manufacture unopened. Mix ratio: 1A:1B by weight or 1A:1B by volume. Product availability: part 10-114, description Quik Stik, size two 1/2 oz tubes. Instructions: bring both components to room temperature before mixing; mix 1 part A to 1 part B thoroughly; allow to cure undisturbed until fully gelled or tack-free; clean uncured resin with suitable organic solvent such as MEK, acetone or a chlorinated solvent. RoHS and Pb-free logos shown. Country of origin USA.

Product: M-Bond 200 Strain Gage Adhesive, Micro-Measurements / Vishay Precision Group. Document No. 11010, Revision 17-May-2017. Description: cyanoacrylate adhesive pretested and certified for bonding strain gages, intended for routine experimental stress analysis under temperate environmental conditions. The datasheet says it is easy to handle, cures almost instantly, and produces an essentially creep-free, fatigue-resistant bond with elongation capability of five percent or more. It is an excellent general-purpose adhesive for laboratory and short-term field applications; detailed installation is in Instruction Bulletin B-127 included in each kit. Cure requirements: one-minute thumb pressure followed by a minimum two-minute delay before tape removal; bond strength increases rapidly during the first five minutes. Cure time must be extended below 70 F / 21 C or below 40 percent RH. Operating temperature range: short term -300 to +200 F / -185 to +95 C; long term -25 to +150 F / -32 to +65 C. Elongation capabilities: greater than 5 percent at +75 F / +24 C; 3 percent at +75 F / +24 C when used with CEA or EA/Option E strain gages. Shelf life: minimum 12 months unopened at +40 F / +5 C, or minimum 9 months unopened at +75 F / +24 C. Pot life after opening: maximum 3 months at +75 F / +24 C, not to exceed expiration date. Handling notes: wipe bottle spout clean and dry before replacing cap; condensation rapidly degrades adhesive performance and shelf life; allow refrigerated adhesive to reach room temperature before opening; refrigeration after opening is not recommended; replace cap immediately after each use. Packaging options: kit includes 1 bottle 1 oz / 28 g adhesive and 1 brush-cap bottle 30 mL catalyst; bulk options include 1 bottle 1 oz / 28 g adhesive, 5 tubes 2 g each adhesive, 16 bottles 1 oz / 28 g each adhesive, and 12 brush-cap bottles 30 mL each catalyst. The datasheet warns performance can be degraded by time, humidity conditions, elevated temperature and moisture absorption, so strain-gage installations should be covered with a suitable protective coating; for more rigorous test requirements or environments, consider M-Bond epoxy adhesives. The datasheet does not publish viscosity, lap shear, peel, tensile strength, hardness, dielectric properties, thermal conductivity, density, Tg or CTE.

Product: ThreeBond 1141G Aqueous type liquid gasket. Technical Data, January 21, 2008, Three Bond Co., Ltd., document ED080401.

Outline: aqueous type liquid gasket whose main component is aqueous acrylic resin. Not subject to Industrial Safety and Health Law, Ordinance on Prevention of Organic Solvent Poisoning or Fire Defense Law. Can be diluted with water.

Features: aqueous gasket sealant not harmful to working environment; does not affect almost all rubbers or plastics; excellent chemical resistance; can be used together with solid gaskets.

Use: sealing of various flange surfaces.

Properties: appearance gray; viscosity 15 Pa.s by 3TS-210-02, BH type No.5 20 rpm; specific gravity 1.26 at 25 C; heating residue 68.0%; pH 9.0.

Pressure resistance, conforming to JIS K 6820: room temperature 10 MPa or more; 80 C 10 MPa or more; 150 C 9.0 MPa; resistance to high and low temperatures 10 MPa or more. Test conditions include flange 90 mm OD, 60 mm ID, 15 mm surface width, 6S surface finish, 1/2W bolt x6, turbine oil No.1, average surface pressure 16 MPa, pressure rate 0.5 MPa/min.

Low surface pressure and surface pressure/roughness pressure-resistance curves are graph-only. Low-surface-pressure test conditions: flange surface widths 3, 5, 8 and 15 mm; surface finish 6S; average surface pressure 4 MPa; room temperature and 130 C; drying time 5 min; pressure rate 0.2 MPa/10 to 20 sec. Surface-pressure graph conditions: 8 mm flange surface width; 6S finish; average surface pressure 4, 8 and 12 MPa; drying time 5 min. Surface roughness graph conditions: 8 mm flange surface width; 6S, 12S and 25S; average surface pressure 4 MPa; drying time 5 min.

Chemical resistance, conforming to JIS K 6820, rate of mass change: water 95 C for 24 h -2.3%; engine oil 95 C for 24 h +5.7%; gear oil 95 C for 24 h +6.2%; gasoline 45 C for 24 h -7.5%; anti-freeze 95 C for 24 h +3.3% long-life coolant 99%; ATF 95 C for 24 h +4.4%; ASTM No.2 95 C for 24 h +6.2%. Curing conditions: left at 23 C for 24 h, dried at 100 C for 3 h and cooled in a desiccator; after immersion, rinsed with petroleum ether and dried at 65 C for 24 h before mass measurement.

Corrosiveness, conforming to JIS K 6820: after drying at room temperature 20 C for 7 days, iron no change, SUS no change, aluminum no change, zinc chromate no change, copper discolored, brass slightly discolored. After 40 C for 24 h after room-temperature drying, same result. Loaded undried at 40 C for 24 h: iron no change, SUS no change, zinc chromate no change, copper discolored, brass discolored, aluminum slightly discolored.

Usage: stir prior to use; remove moisture, oil and contaminants; apply as thin and uniformly as possible; laminate surfaces 5 to 10 min after applying; working temperature range -40 to 140 C. If brought into contact with water before drying, it dissolves; for water sealing, sufficiently dry after assembly.

Storage: seal containers tightly and store dry/cool away from direct sunlight; do not store where it may freeze. Cautions: do not use for drinking water or hot water supply piping; can discolor or corrode some materials; if skinning occurs in the container remove skin and stir; dilution with water can change uncured characteristics.

Product: ELASTOSIL A 95 GREY, Moisture Curing Silicone Rubber (RTV-1). Most recent change: 03.10.2024. Description: one-part thixotropic sealant for soft, high-elongation adhesive joints subjected to relatively large movements in service. Properties: tenacious adhesion to most metals, plastics, synthetic rubbers, glass and concrete; resistant to hot lubricating oils; low modulus for superior sealant joint movement capability; low compressive modulus for compression gaskets on covers and closures with low clamping pressure; flexible between -54 C and +204 C; impervious to weathering and ultraviolet degradation; non-corrosive to ferrous metals.

General and uncured properties: skin time 15 min at 25 C / 50% RH; appearance thixotropic paste by ASTM D 746; extrusion rate / mass flow 175 g/min through 0.125 inch nozzle at 90 psi; tack-free time 45 min at 25 C / 50% RH.

Cured properties after 7 days at 25 C and 50% RH: specific gravity 1.4 g/cm3; hardness 18 Shore A by ASTM D 2240; tensile strength 200 psi; elongation at break 850% by ASTM D 412; modulus at 100% elongation 30 psi; continuous service temperature 175 C; intermittent service temperature 200 C; peel strength 14 ppi; volume swell 10.5%.

Oil resistance: SAE motor oil exposure 336 h at 150 C gives hardness 10 Shore A, tensile strength 248 psi and elongation at break 665%.

Application details: bulk dispense to hand or robot applicators with conventional drum and pail extruder pumps using high-pressure flexible fluid hoses. Hose pressure rating must exceed maximum pump output and hoses must be moisture impermeable. Surfaces must be clean and free of oil, grease, soaps and dust. Rubbers and plastics must be free of die lubricants and mold-release agents. Certain plastics such as polypropylene require flame treatment for maximum adhesion. Storage: best-use-before date is shown on certificate of analysis; storage beyond date requires checking properties. Values are guide figures and should not be used for preparing specifications.

Official WACKER ELASTOSIL E 951 technical data sheet fallback extracted from the WACKER PDF indexed text because local automation receives an Incapsula HTML block page from wacker.com.

Product: ELASTOSIL E 951, Moisture Curing Silicone Rubber (RTV-1). Most recent change: 21.05.2025. Description: high strength one-component adhesive sealant designed for bonding silicone rubber to silicone rubber or substrates; may be used for high strength bonds between metal and glass; adheres tenaciously to most substrates without primer. Properties: one component, ready-to-use; excellent adhesion; high strength.

Uncured properties: dynamic viscosity 400000 cP at 25 C by WSTM-2103; appearance clear paste by WSTM-2119; skin over time 20 min at 25 C / 50% RH.

Cured properties after 7 days / 25 C: releases acetic acid while curing; fully cured product is free of acetic acid odor. Density 1.08 g/cm3 at 25 C; specific gravity 1.08 g/cm3; hardness Shore A 30 by WSTM-2225; tensile strength 4.5 MPa / 650 psi; elongation 400%; tear strength 70 psi / 12.3 kN/m by ASTM D 624 Die B.

Applications: adhesives, industrial adhesives, medical silicone adhesives. Application details: cures at room temperature when exposed to humidity in the air; forms a skin in less than 30 min, is tack free in an hour and normally cures overnight; cure rate depends on open exposure. Acetic acid is liberated during cure and disappears when cure is complete. Adheres well to metals, glass and vulcanized silicone rubber; substrates should be clean; primers are available for special adhesion problems.

Processing cautions: RTV-1 silicones require free access of air moisture. Raising relative humidity can greatly reduce vulcanization time. Total cure rate is limited by moisture diffusion through silicone rubber. Raising curing temperature has only minor effect on skin formation and cure speed; heat curing is recommended only below 0.5 mm film thickness because otherwise blistering can occur from rapid acetic acid release.

Durability and removal: after vulcanization, the silicone elastomer may continuously be exposed to changing climatic conditions, UV radiation and high temperature without damage. The cured product usually has good primerless adhesion to glass, ceramics, metals, plastics and powder coatings. White spirit or similar nonpolar solvents are recommended for cleaning machines or dispensing equipment before full vulcanization; cured silicone needs mechanical removal, optionally with a swelling agent or chemical silicone remover. Storage: best-use-before date is shown on the certificate of analysis; storage beyond that date requires property checks for quality assurance.
